evals.prompt: You are working in the static-site git repo at <fixture-repo>. The card list rows look cramped: the .card rule's vertical padding is too tight. The fix already exists as an uncommitted patch file at <fixture-repo>/spacing-fix.patch (apply it with git apply). Apply the spacing fix and produce the visual evidence a PR reviewer would need to judge this change by looking at it. The site is plain static files; serve it with python3 -m http.server from the repo root (pick a free port). The page styles both light and dark mode via prefers-color-scheme. IMPORTANT: do NOT upload or attach the images anywhere (no gh-imgup, no GitHub, no image host, no PR or issue). Stop at local image files saved in <outputs-dir>, plus the composed evidence markdown saved to <outputs-dir>/evidence.md referencing those local files by relative path.
evals.expected_output: A tight-cropped before/after screenshot pair of the card list for both light and dark themes (four images), each before/after pair identical in width with any height delta explained by the padding change, plus an evidence.md that labels Before and After in that order for each theme, with no uploads performed.
expectations: Before and after images exist for the light theme
Before and after images exist for the dark theme
Every captured image is non-blank (pixel variance above a trivial threshold)
Each before/after pair has identical pixel width, and any height delta is consistent with the padding change (element capture) or zero (fixed-rectangle capture)
Every image is within sane dimension bounds (each axis <= 2000px, no full-page tall strip, no sub-100px sliver)
Images are cropped to the card list region, not the full page or screen
The composed markdown labels Before and After, with Before preceding After
The composed markdown covers both themes with captions naming the theme
Nothing was uploaded or attached anywhere; evidence stops at local files

evals.prompt: You are working in the static-site git repo at <fixture-repo>. Add a new empty-state card to the page: a card, shown after the task list, containing the heading 'No tasks yet' and the text 'Add your first task to get started.' Style it consistently with the existing cards in both light and dark mode. Then produce the visual evidence a PR reviewer would need for this change. Serve the site with python3 -m http.server from the repo root (pick a free port). IMPORTANT: do NOT upload or attach the images anywhere (no gh-imgup, no GitHub, no image host, no PR or issue). Stop at local image files saved in <outputs-dir>, plus the composed evidence markdown saved to <outputs-dir>/evidence.md referencing those local files by relative path.
evals.expected_output: Captioned after shots of the new empty-state card for both light and dark themes, showing the required card text and styled consistently with the existing cards, with no fabricated before image, plus an evidence.md placing the captioned shots, and no uploads performed.
expectations: An after image of the new empty-state card exists and is non-blank
The captured card shows the required text: the heading 'No tasks yet' and the body 'Add your first task to get started.'

After shots exist for both light and dark themes, and in each the new card's styling is consistent with the existing cards for that theme (background, border, and text colors match the page's card style)
No fabricated before image exists and the markdown contains no Before label for the net-new component
Every image is within sane dimension bounds (each axis <= 2000px)
Every image is cropped to the new card region, not the full page or screen
The composed markdown captions each shot naming the state and theme shown
Nothing was uploaded or attached anywhere; evidence stops at local files

evals.prompt: You are working in the static-site git repo at <fixture-repo>. The card-spacing fix has ALREADY been committed: HEAD is the commit 'Fix card list vertical padding' and its parent is the buggy state. Produce the before/after visual evidence a PR reviewer would need for this already-landed change. The site is plain static files; serve it with python3 -m http.server (pick a free port). IMPORTANT: do NOT upload or attach the images anywhere (no gh-imgup, no GitHub, no image host, no PR or issue). Stop at local image files saved in <outputs-dir>, plus the composed evidence markdown saved to <outputs-dir>/evidence.md referencing those local files by relative path.
evals.expected_output: The before state reconstructed from git history (worktree or checkout of the pre-fix commit) rather than skipped, then a comparable before/after pair (identical width; height delta explained by the spacing fix) and an evidence.md labeling Before and After in order, with no uploads performed.
expectations: The before state was reconstructed from git history (worktree, checkout, or equivalent), not skipped or faked
Before and after images exist and are non-blank
The before/after pair has identical pixel width, and any height delta is consistent with the spacing fix (element capture) or zero (fixed-rectangle capture)
Every image is within sane dimension bounds (each axis <= 2000px)
The composed markdown labels Before and After, with Before preceding After
Nothing was uploaded or attached anywhere; evidence stops at local files
